obligations.reason: apb_soc_ctrl exposes SoC boot, fetch-enable, reset, power, cluster, mux, and JTAG-related outputs through an APB slave; these are protected control assets if reachable from unprivileged or debug/peripheral bus subjects.
obligations.subject: APB/peripheral bus requester
obligations.object: fc_bootaddr_o, fc_fetchen_o, cluster_boot_addr_o, cluster_fetch_enable_o, cluster_rstn_o, cluster_pow_o, cluster_byp_o, sel_hyper_axi_o, soc_jtag_reg_o
obligations.expected_guard: writes to SoC control/security-sensitive boot, fetch, reset, power, mux, and JTAG-visible registers should be mediated by an explicit privilege/lock/lifecycle/debug-authorization condition, not only PSEL/PENABLE/PWRITE/address decode
obligations.candidate_path: APB/peripheral bus decode into soc_ctrl register file
state_conditions_to_check: after reset before trusted firmware lock/setup
after any lock bit is set, if present
when APB access originates from debug/JTAG/peripheral path if distinguishable
required_evidence_slots: subject APB master or bus source reaching s_soc_ctrl_bus
address decode for each protected register
presence/absence and placement of privilege lock lifecycle or debug authorization guard
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer lists ports and instances but not register decode or access-control expressions; inspect RTL to classify actual guards.

obligations.reason: Visible RTL has s_apb_debug_bus, s_lint_debug_bus, s_lint_jtag_bus, i_lint_jtag, and soc_jtag_reg ports; debug presence is not itself a bug, so check whether reachable protected objects are guarded by authorization.
obligations.subject: JTAG/debug interface requester
obligations.operation: read/write/expose/override through debug or JTAG path
obligations.object: soc_jtag_reg_i/o, APB debug bus, lint_jtag/debug paths, memory/peripheral targets reachable through interconnect
obligations.expected_guard: JTAG/debug initiated accesses or register updates should require debug authorization/lifecycle enable and should not bypass normal APB/interconnect access mediation for protected SoC state or memory
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG/debug buses and JTAG register path into SoC control or local interconnect
state_conditions_to_check: during APB or local interconnect arbitration
when normal CPU access would be restricted
required_evidence_slots: target object or address range reached
read/write path through bus/interconnect or soc_ctrl register
obligations.uncertainty: The fact layer names debug/JTAG buses but omits lint_jtag_wrap internals; inspect available top-level connections and interconnect mediation.

obligations.reason: apb_fll_if has three FLL request/data/write channels and lock inputs; verify lock inputs actually mediate APB writes to the associated FLL targets and all decoded write cases.
obligations.subject: APB requester via s_fll_bus
obligations.object: fll1_add/data/wrn/req, fll2_add/data/wrn/req, fll3_add/data/wrn/req and associated FLL configuration state
obligations.expected_guard: FLL configuration writes should be blocked or restricted when the corresponding fll*_lock is asserted and should not allow clearing/bypassing lock-controlled state through alternate request paths
obligations.candidate_path: FLL APB interface to FLL control registers and lock status
state_conditions_to_check: pending request/ack handshake while lock changes
required_evidence_slots: behavior for reads of locked state
absence/presence of alternate writes not covered by lock
obligations.uncertainty: Need RTL decode and handshake review; locks are visible only as ports in facts.

obligations.reason: apb_node routes a shared APB requester to multiple peripheral slaves; protected APB peripherals such as soc_ctrl/FLL/uDMA require correct range mediation and no unintended aliases.
obligations.subject: shared APB bus requester
obligations.operation: route APB read/write to peripheral slaves
obligations.object: s_fll_bus, s_gpio_bus, s_udma_bus, s_soc_ctrl_bus, s_adv_timer_bus, s_soc_evnt_gen_bus, s_stdout_bus, s_apb_timer_bus
obligations.expected_guard: slave selection should enforce exact address ranges and prevent overlapping aliases from routing unprivileged/debug APB accesses to protected peripherals
obligations.candidate_path: APB node/peripheral address decoding from shared bus to slave selects
state_conditions_to_check: boundary address at each peripheral range
overlapping high/low address bits if truncation occurs
debug APB vs normal APB source if separate
required_evidence_slots: input subject bus and address width
handling of unmapped addresses and PSLVERR/PRDATA
top-level mapping of each psel to slave
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer is truncated for some ports and does not include parameters/range constants; inspect include files and decode logic.

obligations.reason: The top-level includes multiple local interconnect masters (debug, JTAG, uDMA TX/RX, FC data/instr) and memory/peripheral targets; permission flaws often arise when one master bypasses range protections applied to another.
obligations.subject: FC data/instruction, uDMA TX/RX, debug, JTAG local bus masters
obligations.object: s_mem_rom_bus, s_scm_l2_data_bus, s_scm_l2_instr_bus, peripheral/APB windows, L2/SCM memory regions
obligations.expected_guard: address decoding and any memory protection/range checks should consistently mediate read and write access for every master, including debug/JTAG and uDMA paths
obligations.candidate_path: SoC interconnect paths from CPU, debug/JTAG, uDMA, and local buses to ROM/L2/SCM/peripherals
required_evidence_slots: list of interconnect masters and slave targets
presence/absence of per-master access matrix/PMP-like checks
obligations.uncertainty: Need RTL inspection to determine whether protection is intended or present; facts only show bus instances.

obligations.reason: uDMA is both APB-configured and an interconnect master; DMA can bypass CPU privilege if address ranges and active-transfer updates are not mediated.
obligations.subject: APB requester configuring uDMA; uDMA master issuing transfers
obligations.operation: configure DMA and issue memory reads/writes
obligations.object: uDMA RX/TX local interconnect buses and APB-configured DMA channel address/size/control state
obligations.expected_guard: uDMA channel configuration and generated transfers should be restricted by address range/access-policy checks and should not allow configuration updates during active transfers to bypass bounds
obligations.candidate_path: uDMA subsystem configuration and active transfer address generation
required_evidence_slots: APB config registers controlling uDMA address/size/enable
write enable and any privilege/lock guard
behavior when config changes while busy/active
error or block behavior for invalid ranges
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer identifies udma_subsystem and local buses but not channel internals; inspect module RTL.

obligations.reason: GPIO can externally influence SoC pins and interrupts; if any pins are security-sensitive or reserved, APB writes should be constrained rather than globally writable.
obligations.subject: APB requester via s_gpio_bus
obligations.expected_guard: writes that change external pin drive direction/output or interrupt behavior should be mediated by privilege/lock/pad-ownership policy where those pins can affect protected signals
obligations.candidate_path: GPIO APB register writes to external pin direction/output and interrupt configuration
state_conditions_to_check: when pin is reserved or assigned to alternate function
required_evidence_slots: APB decode for GPIO control registers
top-level mapping of GPIO pins to external/security-sensitive functions
obligations.uncertainty: Need top-level pad mapping and register decode; obligation is conditional on protected/reserved GPIO usage.

obligations.reason: Clock, reset, FLL, timer, and power controls can affect availability and security lifecycle; verify APB write paths include intended authorization or safe-state conditions.
obligations.subject: APB requester
obligations.object: cluster_rstn_o, cluster_pow_o, clock divider outputs, FLL control outputs, timer busy/irq/event control
obligations.expected_guard: software-visible clock, reset, divider, and power control updates should require privilege/lock or safe-state checks when they can disable, reset, or destabilize protected domains
obligations.candidate_path: clock/reset/power control registers in soc_ctrl, clkdiv, timer and FLL interfaces
required_evidence_slots: effect on protected domains or outputs
obligations.uncertainty: This is a policy-oriented obligation; vulnerability depends on whether controlled domains are protected assets in this SoC.

obligations.reason: Visible interfaces include DFT clock gating, BIST, clock-gate, bypass, and mux controls; test capabilities can bypass normal operation if reachable without lifecycle authorization.
obligations.subject: test/DFT interface, APB/debug requester if connected
obligations.object: dft_cg_enable_i, bist_en, bist_pattern, clkgate_* controls, cluster_byp_o, bbmux_* controls
obligations.expected_guard: test, DFT clock-gate enable, BIST enable/pattern, and bypass/mux controls should be gated by lifecycle/test-mode authorization and not be writable or asserted from normal APB/debug paths without mediation
obligations.candidate_path: test/DFT/BIST and clock-gating control signals from interfaces and peripherals
state_conditions_to_check: debug active while test controls asserted
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer shows interfaces and ports, not all signal drivers; trace connectivity in top-level RTL.
